articles.decoder: * **Deep Work**: Professional activity performed in a state of distraction-free concentration that pushes cognitive capabilities to their limit.

articles.title: The Naturally Refreshing Visual Language of Sour Soda Studio
articles.description: Sour Soda Studio is an anonymous side project by an established illustrator known as A, built over four years through experimentation with freehand vector brushes in Adobe Fresco, resulting in a soft, rubber-like visual language of organic lines and flat colors.
articles.one-liner: Illustrator 'A' launched an anonymous side project called Sour Soda Studio, utilizing freehand vector brushes in Adobe Fresco to create organic, rubbery-looking digital art.

summary.what: The studio features a portfolio centered on nature and sustainability, built over four years by an established illustrator seeking a departure from their previous commercial work. The style relies on 'vector-based engraving' using Adobe Fresco brushes and avoids sharp edges in favor of clay-like forms.
summary.why: This project highlights how professional artists use anonymous side projects as a 'creative sandbox' to experiment with new visual languages that differ from the established styles requested by their commercial clients.

articles.decoder:
* **Adobe Fresco**: A digital drawing and painting app that supports both pixel and live vector brushes.
* **Vector-based**: Graphics defined by mathematical equations rather than fixed grids of pixels, allowing them to be scaled infinitely without loss of quality.

articles.title: From Beginner to Pro Artist in Three Years: The Five Art Lessons that Made it Possible
articles.description: Morgan Noll went from complete novice to professional animation artist in just three years, landing a role on Star Trek: Lower Decks.
articles.one-liner: Morgan Noll transitioned from a theatre worker with no art background to a professional background painter for 'Star Trek: Lower Decks' in three years.

summary.what: Noll used the 2020 lockdown to self-teach through platforms like Drawabox, Schoolism, and Underpaint Academy, ultimately landing a job at animation studio Titmouse. She credits her progress to mastering specific concepts like linking colors through greys, independent vanishing points for non-grid objects, and atmospheric perspective.
summary.why: The democratization of specialized art education through online platforms like Schoolism and Drawabox has significantly lowered the barrier to entry for professional roles in competitive animation studios.
summary.takeaway: If you are learning perspective, study the 'Space Drawing: Perspective' book by Dongho Kim to understand how object surface area changes as it recedes into the background.

articles.deep-summary:
* **Saturation Control**: Noll uses a neutral grey in the center of her palette, flanked by two saturated hues, to create high-impact focus areas.
* **Perspective Logic**: Every object in a scene can have its own vanishing points along a shared horizon line, which is crucial for painting tilted or non-aligned objects.
* **Material Physics**: Lighting for both matte and chrome objects follows the same formula; the difference in surface finish is determined by the 'roughness' or 'diffusion' settings of the light treatment.
* **Atmospheric Distance**: Artists can simulate distance by using the sky's color and value to paint the air volume between the viewer and the object.
* **Surface Area Scaling**: Correctly diminishing the surface area of objects as they retreat is more critical for grounding a scene than simply changing object height or width.
articles.decoder:
* **Background Painter**: An artist responsible for creating the environments and scenery in an animated production.
* **Vanishing Point**: A point in a perspective drawing where parallel lines appear to converge at the horizon line.
* **Chroma**: The purity or intensity of a color.

articles.title: Ikea turns everyday objects into ingenious World Cup guessing game
articles.description: Ikea Canada's "Assemble the World" campaign creatively celebrates the 2026 World Cup by using everyday Ikea products to recreate national flags, blending interactive marketing with the brand's signature design aesthetic.
articles.one-liner: Ikea Canada is promoting the 2026 World Cup by featuring national flags constructed entirely from common household products in a social-first marketing campaign.

summary.what: The 'Assemble the World' campaign, created with Dentsu Creative, challenges social media followers to identify the Ikea products (like the VÅGSJÖN blanket or PEPPRIG duster cover) used to build 18 different national flags. The campaign will also be featured in out-of-home (OOH) advertising near retail locations throughout the summer.
summary.why: Brands are increasingly using 'interactive visual puzzles' on social media to build organic engagement during major sporting events, moving away from traditional broadcast spots.

articles.decoder: * **OOH**: Out-of-home advertising, referring to billboards, posters, or digital displays placed in public spaces.
